div.p: Let everyone who shall have seen or heard this document know that I, Isabella the spouse of Robert of Pollok, being determined in my own will, by the consent of Robert [my] husband and the advice of Robert Crock my father, have given and granted to Simon of Lindsay at perpetual ferme my marriage dowry which I have in the territory of Mow, that is ‘Hungerig’ with all its pertinents, freedoms, and easements, just as the same land was given to my father by the Lady Eschina of Mow; for him and his heirs or those to whom they might wish to assign it, to hold of me and my heirs free and quit from all service and exaction, by rendering thereupon ten shillings yearly, that is, five at Pentecost and five at the feast of Saint Martin, and to the Lady Eschina and her heirs for me and my heirs one sore sparrow-hawk yearly at the Roxburgh fair; and for this agreement firmly and faithfully to be held, for me and my heirs, I, Isabella, having held sacred relics, have sworn before men of discretion that if [I] and my heirs at any time wish to go against the written agreement, we should pay by way of a penalty one hundred shillings to the said Simon or his attorneys, the forewritten agreement remaining in his ... and since I do not have my own seal for the corroboration of this document, I have applied the seal of my husband and the seal of my father to this document. As witnesses, John the dean of Clydesdale, Philip of Partick, Alan of Ness, William son of Thorald, Hugh Lurgot, Adam Young, and others.
